pages.name: Home
pages.description: Homepage introducing the wedding planning blog and providing quick access to key sections, tools, and summaries of saved items using featured content and shortcut counts.
primary_functions: Show featured articles, tools, and inspiration for wedding planning.
Provide clear navigation entry points to Budget & Money, Venues, Food & Drink, DIY & Decor, Inspiration, and Planning tools.
Surface shortcuts to saved areas like Reading List, My Moodboards, Planning Board, Budget Planner, My Projects, and My To-Dos.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "Budget & Money" in header or a featured budget article section
Click "Venues" in header or a featured venue guide
Click "Food & Drink" in header or a featured catering article
Click "DIY & Decor" in header or a featured DIY project
Click "Inspiration: Color Palettes" in header or a featured palette
Click planning tools teaser for "Timeline Templates"
Click planning tools teaser for "Seating & Floorplans"
Click planning tools teaser for "Checklists"
Click shortcut tile showing reading_list_count
Click shortcut tile showing moodboards_count
Click shortcut tile showing planning_board_items_count
Click shortcut tile showing budget_planner_linked_articles_count
Click shortcut tile showing projects_count
Click shortcut tile showing todos_pending_count
Click on any featured article card

access_methods.description: Default landing page when visiting site root URL

pages.name: Articles List
pages.description: Reusable listing page for article-based categories such as Budget & Money, Venues, and Food & Drink, with category-specific filters and sorting.
primary_functions: Display a list of articles for the selected category (e.g., budget_money, venues, food_drink) with titles, summaries, and key metadata.
Provide category-specific filters such as Overall Budget, Published Date, Guest Count, Venue Style, and Budget per Guest.
Allow sorting of results (e.g., Most Popular, Budget breakdowns first, Newest).
Let users open any article in detail view by clicking its title.

incoming_params.description: Article category to browse: 'budget_money', 'venues', or 'food_drink'. Required for correct filters.
Selected overall budget filter key, e.g., 'under_10000'. Used for Budget & Money lists.
Selected published date range key, e.g., 'last_12_months'.
Guest count range filter key, e.g., 'around_150_guests_120_180', mainly for venue guides.
Comma-separated list of venue style slugs to filter by, e.g., 'barn,hotel_ballroom'.
Per-guest catering budget range key, e.g., '40_60'. Used for Food & Drink.
Sort option value such as 'most_popular', 'newest', or 'budget_breakdowns_first'.
Current page number for pagination.
Number of articles per page.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on an article title or card in the list

access_methods.description: Filters and sorting update the URL query parameters to preserve state and shareable links
Linked from featured sections on the homepage or other pages using categoryId

pages.name: Article Detail
pages.description: Detail view for an individual article, such as budget guides, venue guides, and catering budget breakdowns, including save and budget planner integration.
primary_functions: Show full article content including headings, sections, images, and data tables (e.g., catering budget table).
Provide a Save to reading list or bookmark button to add the article to the Reading List.
For budget-related articles, provide an Add to budget plan button that links the article to the Budget Planner under the relevant category (e.g., Catering).
Highlight key metadata such as category, published date, estimated budgets, and guest count guidance.

incoming_params.description: ID of the article to display in detail.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "View reading list" link after saving an article
Click a "View in budget planner" link after adding a catering article to the budget plan

access_methods.description: Accessed when a user clicks an article from the Articles List, Reading List, Budget Planner, or homepage, passing articleId in the URL

pages.name: Timeline Templates List
pages.description: Listing page for wedding day timeline templates under Planning Tools, with filters for guest count, season, location, length, and ceremony time.
primary_functions: Display a list of timeline templates with key attributes such as length, guest count range, season, location type, and ceremony time.
Provide filters for Guest Count, Season, Location Type, Timeline Length, and Ceremony Time so users can narrow down to specific scenarios (e.g., 8 hours, summer, outdoor, 4:00 PM).
Allow sorting (e.g., Most Popular, Newest).
Enable navigation to a specific timeline templates detail page by clicking its title or card.

incoming_params.description: Guest count range key for filtering timeline templates, e.g., '50_150_guests'.
Selected season value such as 'summer'.
Selected location type such as 'outdoor_ceremony_reception'.
Timeline length in hours, e.g., 8.
Ceremony start time string, e.g., '4:00 PM'.
Sort option for templates, e.g., 'most_popular' or 'newest'.
Current page number for pagination.
Number of templates to show per page.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a timeline template card or title

access_methods.description: Links from homepage planning tools section
Filter and sort selections are reflected in URL parameters for sharable filtered views

pages.name: Timeline Template Detail
pages.description: Detail page for an individual wedding day timeline template, showing the full schedule and save/export actions.
primary_functions: Show a full hour-by-hour (or half-hour) wedding day schedule including ceremony, cocktail hour, reception, and vendor timing.
Provide a prominent Save this timeline button to add the template to the users Planning Board.
Allow basic actions such as printing or exporting the timeline for offline reference.

incoming_params.description: ID of the timeline template to display in detail.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "View on Planning Board" link after successfully saving the timeline

access_methods.description: Accessed from the Timeline Templates List or Planning Board by passing timelineTemplateId in the URL

pages.name: Seating Layouts List
pages.description: Listing page for seating and floorplan layout templates under Planning, with filters for guest count and table types.
primary_functions: Display layout templates with thumbnails and summaries describing table types (round, long) and capacity ranges.
Provide filters such as Guest Count and Layout Type (e.g., Round tables, Long tables, both) so users can find mixed-table layouts for specific guest ranges (e.g., 100150 guests).
Offer sorting options such as Most Used or Most Downloaded.
Open a layouts detail page when the user clicks on a layout title or card.

incoming_params.description: Guest count range key such as '100_150_guests' used to filter layouts.
Comma-separated list of layout type slugs, e.g., 'round_tables,long_tables'.
Sort option: 'most_used' or 'most_downloaded'.
Current page for pagination.
Number of layouts per page.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a seating layout card or title

access_methods.description: Filter selections are encoded in URL parameters

pages.name: Seating Layout Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a specific seating layout or floorplan template, including a detailed chart and save action.
primary_functions: Display a detailed seating chart view, including table shapes, counts, and capacity for each table type.
Provide a View sample seating chart button to view an enlarged or more detailed chart.
Allow the user to save the layout to the Planning Board via a Save layout or Add this layout to my planning board button.

incoming_params.description: ID of the seating layout template to display.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "View on Planning Board" link after saving the seating layout

access_methods.description: Opened from Seating Layouts List or Planning Board by passing seatingLayoutId

pages.name: DIY Projects List
pages.description: Listing page for DIY & Decor project tutorials with filters for time, cost, difficulty, and style.
primary_functions: Display a list of DIY decor projects with preview images, estimated time, cost, and difficulty level.
Provide filters for Time Required (e.g., Under 2 hours), Project Cost (e.g., Under $100), Difficulty (e.g., Beginner), and style tags.
Allow users to open a projects detail/tutorial page by clicking its title or image.

incoming_params.description: Time filter key, e.g., 'under_2_hours'.
Cost filter key, e.g., 'under_100'.
Difficulty filter value such as 'beginner'.
Optional comma-separated list of style tags to filter by.
Current page number for pagination.
Number of projects per page.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a DIY project card or title

access_methods.description: Links from homepage featured DIY projects
Filter state encoded in URL parameters

pages.name: DIY Project Detail
pages.description: Detail/tutorial page for an individual DIY decor project, including materials and step-by-step instructions.
primary_functions: Show full project instructions including materials list, step-by-step directions, and photos.
Provide an Add to project list button to save the project into the users My Projects page.
Highlight estimated time, cost, and difficulty so users can confirm the project matches their constraints.

incoming_params.description: ID of the DIY project to display.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "View My Projects" link after adding a project to the list

access_methods.description: Opened from DIY Projects List, My Projects, or homepage via diyProjectId

pages.name: Color Palettes
pages.description: Inspiration page listing wedding color palettes and enabling creation and population of moodboards.
primary_functions: Display a gallery of color palette cards with swatch previews and names.
Provide tag-based filters (e.g., Sage Green, Gold) and Palette Type filters (e.g., Includes Neutrals) to narrow palettes.
Offer a Create New Moodboard button that prompts for a name (e.g., Sage & Gold) and creates a new moodboard.
Allow users to add a palette to a selected moodboard using an Add to moodboard or + action on each palette card.

incoming_params.description: Comma-separated list of palette tags to filter by, e.g., 'Sage Green,Gold'.
Palette type filter value, e.g., 'includes_neutrals'.
Current page of palettes for pagination.
Number of palettes to show per page.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "My Moodboards" link in header from this page
Click a "View moodboard" link immediately after creating a new moodboard

access_methods.description: Linked from homepage featured color palettes
Filter state (tags, paletteType) encoded in query parameters

pages.name: Moodboards List
pages.description: Hub page listing all moodboards created by the user, with options to manage and open each in detail.
primary_functions: List all existing moodboards with names, thumbnail previews, and counts of palettes/items.
Let users open a specific moodboards detail view (e.g., the Sage & Gold moodboard).
Provide basic management actions such as renaming or deleting moodboards if desired.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a moodboard card or name
Click a "Find palettes" or "Add palettes" CTA from this page

pages.name: Moodboard Detail
pages.description: Detail view of a single moodboard showing all associated palettes and inspiration, with options to manage them.
primary_functions: Display all color palettes and inspiration items saved to this moodboard in a visually organized layout.
Allow users to remove palettes from the moodboard or reorder them for presentation.
Provide a clear moodboard title and optional description so the user can confirm they have successfully added selected palettes (e.g., four sage green and gold palettes).

incoming_params.description: ID of the moodboard to display.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click an "Add more palettes" button

access_methods.description: Opened from Moodboards List or Color Palettes page with moodboardId in URL

pages.name: Checklists
pages.description: Planning Tools page for generating customized wedding planning checklists based on timeline, budget, and guest count.
primary_functions: Provide filters for Timeline (e.g., 6 months), Budget (e.g., Under $15,000), and Guest Count (e.g., 80120 guests) to configure a checklist.
Generate and display a customized checklist when the user clicks Generate checklist or View checklist.
Organize tasks into timeline sections (e.g., This Month / Month 1, Month 2) and show the first months tasks clearly.
Allow users to add individual tasks to their central to-do list via an Add to my to-dos button or checkbox next to each task.

incoming_params.description: Selected planning timeline length in months, e.g., 6.
Selected budget option key, e.g., 'under_15000'.
Guest count range key, e.g., '80_120_guests'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "View My To-Dos" link after adding tasks from the checklist

access_methods.description: "Generate checklist" button submits selected filter values to generateChecklist

pages.name: My To-Dos
pages.description: Centralized task list aggregating tasks added from checklists and other tools, with status and detail controls.
primary_functions: List all tasks the user has added from checklists or other planning tools.
Allow users to mark tasks as complete, edit basic details (such as due dates or notes), and remove tasks.
Provide a view that confirms tasks from the most recent checklist generation (e.g., the first five tasks from the This Month section) have been added.

incoming_params.description: Optional query indicating the origin of newly added tasks, e.g., 'latest_checklist', to visually highlight them.

access_methods.description: Linked from checklist confirmation messages and homepage shortcuts

pages.name: Budget Planner
pages.description: Tool for managing the overall wedding budget, category allocations, and linked reference articles like catering budget breakdowns.
primary_functions: Show a budget overview grouped by expense categories (e.g., Catering, Venue, Decor) including planned vs. actual amounts.
Display references to any budget articles added from Article Detail pages, especially within the Catering section when articles under $60/guest are added.
Allow users to adjust category budgets, remove linked articles, and annotate how they plan to allocate funds.

incoming_params.description: Optional category slug (e.g., 'catering') to scroll to and highlight when the page loads.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a linked budget article title within a category

access_methods.description: Linked from budget-focused articles and homepage shortcuts

pages.name: Planning Board
pages.description: Visual board for saved planning assets like timelines and seating layouts, giving a centralized view of day-of logistics.
primary_functions: Display saved timeline templates and seating layouts that the user has added from their respective detail pages.
Allow users to open any saved item in its detail view (timeline or seating layout).
Provide options to remove items from the Planning Board once they are no longer needed.

incoming_params.description: Optional PlanningBoardItem ID to highlight after saving an item.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a saved timeline item card
Click on a saved seating layout item card

access_methods.description: Linked from confirmation states on timeline and seating layout detail pages

pages.name: Reading List
pages.description: Saved reading list for articles including budget guides, venue guides, and other long-form content, with simple management controls.
primary_functions: List all articles the user has saved from Article Detail pages (e.g., budget wedding guides, venue guides that mention backup indoor space).
Allow users to open any saved article in the Article Detail view.
Offer simple management actions such as removing items from the reading list and optionally filtering or grouping by category.

incoming_params.description: Optional category filter for the reading list, such as 'budget_money', 'venues', or 'food_drink'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a saved article title
Click a "Browse more in this category" link from within the reading list

access_methods.description: Linked from article save confirmations and homepage shortcut

pages.name: My Projects
pages.description: Dedicated page listing all DIY & decor projects the user has saved, with basic status management.
primary_functions: Display all DIY projects added via the Add to project list button from DIY Project Detail pages.
Allow users to open project tutorials again, as well as mark projects as planned, in progress, or completed if desired.
Provide the ability to remove projects from the list when they are no longer relevant.

incoming_params.description: Optional saved project ID to highlight after adding a new project.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a saved project title to re-open its tutorial

access_methods.description: Linked from DIY Project Detail confirmation and homepage shortcuts

header_links.description: Homepage with featured content and quick links.
Articles on budgeting, saving, and sample budgets for weddings.
Venue guides and inspiration, filterable by guest count and style.
Food and catering articles including per-guest budget breakdowns.
DIY decor tutorials and project ideas.
Color palette inspiration and moodboard creation.
Wedding day timeline templates with filters for guest count, season, and timing.
Seating layout and floorplan templates for different guest counts and table types.
Overall wedding budget overview and linked reference articles.
Saved timelines and seating layouts in one board.
Saved articles and guides for later reading.
List of all saved moodboards including color palettes.

footer_links.description: View and adjust your wedding budget.
Review saved timelines and seating layouts.
Go to your wedding planning task list.
